---
title: Econ490_ControlVar_Analysis_2
date: '2024'
description: NA
categories:
  - Python
format:
  html: default
  ipynb:
    jupyter:
      kernelspec:
        display_name: Python
        language: python3
        name: python3
jupyter:
  jupytext:
    text_representation:
      extension: .qmd
      format_name: quarto
      format_version: '1.0'
      jupytext_version: 1.16.1
  kernelspec:
    display_name: Python 3 (ipykernel)
    language: Python
    name: python3
---
```{python}

import pandas as pd

# Path to the files
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Open the controls  file
controls_data = pd.read_excel(f"{path}/Controls_Dataset_2.xlsx")

print("\nFirst few rows of Controls_Dataset_2.xlsx:")
print(controls_data.head())

```



```{python}

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

# Path to the file
path = r"C:/Users/alexr/OneDrive/Desktop/WORK/490_thesis/Final"

# Open the controls file
controls_data = pd.read_excel(f"{path}/Controls_Dataset_2.xlsx")

# List of variables to analyze
variables = ['Inflation', 'Inflation_annual_change', 'S&P_Yearly_Growth', 'Federal_Funds_Rate_Average_Yield']

# Calculate statistics for each variable
for var in variables:
    if var in controls_data.columns:
        mean = controls_data[var].mean()
        std = controls_data[var].std()
        min_val = controls_data[var].min()
        max_val = controls_data[var].max()
        
        print(f"\nStatistics for {var}:")
        print(f"Mean: {mean:.4f}")
        print(f"Standard Deviation: {std:.4f}")
        print(f"Minimum: {min_val:.4f}")
        print(f"Maximum: {max_val:.4f}")
    else:
        print(f"\nVariable '{var}' not found in the dataset.")

# Display summary statistics using describe() method
print("\nSummary Statistics:")
print(controls_data[variables].describe())

```
